page: Microsoft Azure DC16ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCADCCV5 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 614 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% under stress-ng testing. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and software compilation speeds fall within the average range. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached write bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested systems. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA extensions.

This server configuration provides a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations. The inclusion of 614 GB of local storage offers qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring fast local scratch space without the added expense of external storage attachments. However, the lack of AVX-512 instructions and the lower uncached memory write speeds represent key tradeoffs for memory-intensive write operations or advanced vector computing.
description: Microsoft Azure DC16ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCADCCV5 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core operations, software compilation, and database benchmarks perform in the average tier. A notable hardware limitation is the weak uncached memory write bandwidth, which falls into the bottom 25%. This instance is suitable for general-purpose computing, web serving, and database workloads requiring local storage, offering a balanced resource profile without the need for specialized GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, delivering balanced compute and storage performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC16ads_cc_v5 is an AMD EPYC-powered general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB of local storage.
tagline: An AMD EPYC general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores, balanced memory density, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor
64.0 GB of system memory and 614 GB of Premium Storage capable local disk
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation speeds
Average multi-core performance, database operations, and web serving throughput
Weak large-block uncached memory write bandwidth as a performance tradeoff
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database applications
